Facts.text: Michael IV, Paphlagonicus, Byzantium Emperor dies of results of dropsy. His wife Empress Zoe elevates her adoptive son to the throne of the Eastern Roman Empire as Michael V.
League of the kingdom signed (covenant against Venice)
New York Governor Lovelace announces monthly mail service between New York & Boston
Massachusetts Bay becomes first American colonial government to issue paper money
First part (number) of the Encyclopedia Britannica is published in Edinburgh, Scotland
Metric system is first adopted in France
English bare-knuckle boxer Tom Cribb beats African-American Tom Molineaux in 33rd of 40 round bout at Copthall Common, England; first interracial boxing match
Dutch regain Sumatra from the British
Mississippi admitted as 20th state of the Union
"Spirit of the Times" begins publishing in New York City, the premier sports journal of the 19th century
Emory College (now Emory University) is chartered in Oxford, Georgia
The first American chapter of Kappa Sigma is founded at the University of Virginia.
Women suffrage (right to vote) granted in Wyoming Territory (US 1st)
Russo-Turkish War: Turkish commander Osman Pasha surrenders the city of Plevna, Ottoman Empire (now Pleven, Bulgaria) to Romanian  Col. Mihail Cerchez
Austria-Hungary, Italy and Great Britain sign Balkan military treaty
Alfred Jarry's play "Ubu Roi" premieres in Paris
The first western pilgrims welcomed at The House of `Abdu'lláh Páshá
-15] British "Black Week" due to defeats in South Africa
Battle at Storm Berge South Africa - Boers vs British army
Delta Sigma Phi fraternity founded at City College of New York, to spread "the principles of friendship and brotherhood among college men, without respect to race or creed."
First Nobel Peace Prizes awarded, first recipients Red Cross founder Jean Henri Dunant and peace activist Frederic Passy
Women are given the right to vote in Tasmania
Founding of Pi Kappa Phi Fraternity in Charleston, South Carolina
King Peter I of Serbia named nationalist regime
Austrian pacifist and writer Bertha von Suttner becomes the 1st woman to be awarded the Nobel Peace Prize
The worst night of the Brown Dog riots in London, when 1,000 medical students clash with 400 police officers over the existence of a memorial for animals who have been vivisected.
Calbraith Rogers completes 1st crossing of US by airplane in 84 days
Dutch lawyer Tobias Asser receives the Nobel Peace Prize
Belgium head of the International Peace Bureau Henri La Fontaine becomes the first Socialist to win the Nobel Peace Prize
Dutch scientist Heike Kamerlingh Onnes receives Nobel prize for physics
French government returns to Paris
John Heyder becomes president of baseball's National League for the 2nd time
NL votes to ban the spitball's use by all new pitchers
Pilot Ross Smith and his brother, navigator Ken Smith, land their two-engine Vickers Vimy in Australia, winning a £10,000 prize as the first all-Australian crew to fly from England
Canton Bulldogs' future HOF tackle Pete Henry makes longest known NFL drop-kicked field goal, 45 yards
Spanish playwright Jacinto Benavente is awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature in absentia in Stockholm, his speech is given by Count de Torata, the Spanish Ambassador
Polish government of Grabski forms
1st radio broadcast in the Sprinfield area (WCBS)
"Grand Ole Opry" first named as such during Barn Dance radio broadcast, in Nashville, Tennessee
King Rama VII (Prajadhipok) grants Thailand a constitution
The "Great Emu War" ends: Emu's surprising resilience to bullets led to Emu victory over Australian military in Campion district, Western Australia
Fascist dictator of Latvia Ulmanis begins building concentration camp
NFL adopts player waiver rule; applies after 6th game of season
Saint-Adelbert cooperation formed by Catholic elite
MLB Chicago White Sox sell contract ofAl Simmonsto Detroit Tigers for $75,000
Britain replaces King Edward VIII stamp series with KingGeorge VI
Stockholm: physicist PBJ Debije receives Nobel prize for chemistry
William Grant Still's Symphony No. 2 ("Song of a New Race") premieres at the Academy of Music by the Philadelphia Orchestra led by conductor Leopold Stokowski
Ruth Fuller Sasaki, Zen teacher, Rinzai line, enters Zen priesthood
KNVB celebrates 50th anniversary
British anti-offensive in Libya (Sidi Barrani)
British battleship Prince of Wales and battlecruiser Repulse (Force Z) sunk following Japanese aerial attacks off Malaya. 840 men die
Japanese troops landed on northern Luzon in the Philippines
Japanese troops overrun Guam
Adolf Hitlernames Anton Mussert "leader of the Dutch people"
North Africa: 5th German panzer army forms under col-gen von Arnim
British 8th Army (1st Canadian Infantry Division) occupies Orsogna/Ortona Italy
9 Dutch citizens hanged by Nazis
German counterattack at Dillingen bridgehead on the Saar
Aust Services lose 3rd Victory Test Cricket to India by 6 wkts
Preston Tucker reveals plan to produce the Torpedo, a new 150 MPH car
Wolfgang Pauli is awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics "for the discovery of the Exclusion Principle, also called the Pauli Principle"
USSR & Czechoslovakia sign trade agreement
Izhak Ben-Zvi elected 2nd president of Israel
WSLS TV channel 10 in Roanoke, VA (NBC) begins broadcasting
KOMO TV channel 4 in Seattle, WA (ABC) begins broadcasting
WSTV (now WTOV) TV channel 9 in Steubenville-Wheeling, OH (CBS) begins
Philadelphia Phillies purchase Connie Mack Stadium
USAF Lt Col John Stapp travels at 632 mph in a rocket sled at Holloman Air Force Base, New Mexico
Establishment of The People's Movement for the Liberation of Angola, MPLA (from Portuguese: Movimento Popular de Libertação de Angola), left-wing party that has ruled since independence from Portugal
Chinese physicists Chen Ning Yang and Tsung-Dao Lee awarded the Nobel Prize for Physics for their work on parity laws, which created a major breakthrough in particle research
1st domestic (NY-Miami) passenger jet flight-National 707 flew 111
University of Pittsburgh agrees to buy Forbes Field from the MLB Pirates baseball team
Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine awarded to Sir Frank Macfarlane Burnet and Peter Medawar for work on tissue grafting
US performs nuclear test at Carlsbad New Mexico (underground)
USSR & Albania break diplomatic relations
Hunters Point (San Francisco) jitney ends service after 50 years
Zanzibar gains independenence from Great Britain
Dutch ends economic boycott of Rhodesia
Test Cricket debut of Doug Walters v England at the Gabba
Israeli Shmuel Yosef Agnon wins Nobel Prize for literature
Nobel Prize in chemistry awarded to Robert S. Mulliken
Japan's biggest heist, the still-unsolved "300 million yen robbery", occurs in Tokyo
North American Soccer League awards NY & Toronto franchises
William H Rehnquist confirmed as Supreme Court justice
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
European Economic Community calls for a European Parliament
Helios 1 launched by US and Germany; later makes closest flyby of the Sun
Wings release triple album "Wings Over America"
Soyuz 26 carries 2 cosmonauts to Salyut 6 space station
Islanders ends 15 game undefeated streak (12-0-3) to Canadians
Piet Dankert appointed as chairman of European Parliament
Soyuz T-3 returns to Earth
USSR performs underground nuclear test
-13] El Salvador army kills 900
The United Nations General Assembly approves Pakistan's proposal for establishing nuclear free-zone in South Asia.
CDC report on a transfusion recipient who developed AIDS symptoms
Soyuz T-5 returns to Earth, 211 days after take-off
American cytogeneticist Barbara McClintock, aged 81, is the first woman to be solely awarded Nobel prize for Physiology or Medicine for her discovery of genetic transposition in maize
Last NFL game at Shea Stadium; Steelers beat NY Jets 34-7
Raul Alfonsin inaugurated as Argentina's 1st civilian president
1st "planet" outside our solar system discovered
WNSY-AM in Newport News VA returns from WGH
Bill to balance the federal budget passed by US Congress
Atlanta Hawk Dominique Wilkins scores 57 points vs Chicago Bulls
France performs nuclear test
"Nightline" is seen in USSR for 1st time
Washington Capitals 1st NHL scoreless tie, vs Montreal Canadiens
President Gustav Husak of Czechoslovakia, resigns
Hindu-Muslim rebellion in Hyderabad-Aligargh India, 140 die
Soyuz TM-10 lands
Space Shuttle STS 35 (Columbia 11) lands
Muslim ruler of Goa, Yusuf Adil Shah and his Ottoman allies surrender to Portuguese forces led byAfonso de Albuquerquewho puts the Muslim population to the sword
Sea battle at Dungeness: Dutch Lt AdmiralMaarten Trompdefeats an English fleet led by Robert Blake

Isaac Newton's derivation of Kepler's laws from his theory of gravity, contained in the paper “De motu corporum in gyrum” (On the motion of bodies in an orbit), is read to the Royal Society byEdmond Halley.
KingJames IIflees London
Laurence Eusden is appointed British Poet Laureate underGeorge I
Bonnie Prince Charlie's Jacobite army reaches Manchester
Hector Berliozpremieres his oratorio "L'enfance du Christ (The Childhood of Christ)", conducting musicians and soloists from the Opéra-Comique, at the Salle Herz, Paris
US Civil War:General Sherman's Union Army reaches Savannah, Georgia and begins a 12-day siege
Johannes Brahms' choir and orchestra piece "Gesang der Parzen" (Song of the Fates) premieres in Basel
"Adventures of Huckleberry Finn" byMark Twainis first published in the UK and Canada (US Feb 1885, due to printing error)
Frank Wedekind's play "Der Kammersänger" premieres in Berlin
First Nobel Prize in Physics awarded toWilhelm Röntgenfor his discovery of X-rays
German organic chemistEmil Fischeris award the Nobel Prize for Chemistry for his work on sugar and purine syntheses
Opening of theAswan Low Damat the first cataract - first dam across the Nile, then the largest masonry dam in the world
Nobel Prize for physics awarded to Pierre andMarie Curiefor their study of spontaneous radiation
Ivan Pavlovawarded the Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine for work on the physiology of digestion, first Russian to win a Nobel Prize
John William Strutt[Lord Rayleigh] andWilliam Ramsayare presented with the Nobel Prize for Physics for their discovery of Argon
FrenchmanHenri Moissanis presented with the Nobel prize for Chemistry for isolating Fluoride
Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine awarded toCamillo Golgiand Santiago Ramón y Cajal "in recognition of their work on the anatomy of the nervous system"
US PresidentTheodore Rooseveltis the 1st American awarded the Nobel Peace Prize
Rudyard Kiplingreceives the Nobel prize for literature, the first English-language writer to do so
Swedish writerSelma Lagerlöfis the first woman to win the Nobel Prize for Literature
Dutch PhysicistJohannes van der Waalswins the Nobel Prize for physics
Belgium poet and playwrightMaurice Maeterlinckis presented in absentia with the Nobel Prize for Literature
Nobel Prize for Physics awarded toGustaf Dalénfor inventing automatic regulators for gas accumulators for lighthouses and buoys
Bengali poetRabindranath Tagorebecomes the first non-European to be presented with the Nobel Prize for Literature for "Gitanjali"
New York Yankees, Boston Red Sox, and Chicago White Sox, oppose AL resolution accusing league presidentBan Johnsonof overstepping his duties
Nobel Peace Prize awarded to US PresidentWoodrow Wilson
August Kroghis awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for his discovery of the regulation mechanisms of capillaries in skeletal muscle

Nobel prizes awarded to Danish physicistNiels Bohrfor his work on the structure of atoms, Francis William Aston for Chemistry and a Peace Prize toFridtjof Nansenfor his work on behalf of displaced victims of World War I at a ceremony in Copenhagen
Irish playwrightGeorge Bernard Shawawarded the Nobel Prize for Literature
2nd part ofAdolf Hitler's Mein Kampf published
Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine jointly awarded toChristiaan Eijkmanand Sir Frederick Gowland Hopkins for the discovery of vitamins
Austrian AmericanKarl Landsteineris presented with the Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine "for his discovery of human blood groups"
German chemistHans Fischeris awarded the Nobel Prize for Chemistry for his work on haemin
IndianChandrasekhara Venkata Ramanis presented with the Nobel Prize for Physics for work on light scattering - first Asian and non-white to win a Science Nobel
Jane Addamsnamed co-recipient of Nobel Peace Prize; 1st American woman Nobel laureate
Manuel Azañabecomes Prime Minister of Spain with Niceto Zamora as President
Nobel Prize for Physics is presented toPaul DiracandErwin Schrödinger"for the discovery of new productive forms of atomic theory"
James Chadwickis awarded the Nobel Prize for Physics for his discovery of the neutron
MLB Philadelphia Athletics sell infielderJimmie Foxx's contract to Boston Red Sox for $150,000 (about $2.8M in 2020 dollars)
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to Irene Joliot-Curie (daughter of Marie Curie) and her husband Frédéric Joliot for the discovery of artificial radioactivity
Nobel Peace Prize awarded toCarlos Saavedra Lamasfor mediating end to Chaco War between Paraguay and Bolivia, 1st Latin American to win
Italian scientistEnrico Fermireceives the Nobel Prize for Physics (work on reduced radioactivity)
1941 NFL Draft:Tom Harmonfrom University of Michigan first pick by Chicago Bears
An early report of the Holocaust prepared by the Polish government-in-exile, using information obtained byWitold Pilecki, is addressed to UN member states

German/Swiss novelistHermann Hessewins the Nobel Prize in Literature "for his inspired writings which, while growing in boldness and penetration, exemplify the classical humanitarian ideals and high qualities of style"
American physiologistsJoseph Erlangerand Herbert Gasser presented with Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine in Stockholm (awarded 1944), for research into nerve function
UN General Assembly adopts theUniversal Declaration of Human Rights
Ralph Bunche(1st black American) presented the Nobel Peace Prize for mediation in Israel
Linus Paulingwins Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his research into the nature of the chemical bond and its applications
Novel Prize for Literature awarded to Sicilian writerSalvatore Quasimodofor his lyrical poetry
Willard Libbywins the Nobel prize in Chemistry for his work developing carbon-14 dating (radiocarbon dating).
Houston Oilers utility backBilly Cannongains AFL record 373 yards combined rushing/receiving in 48-21 win over NY Titans at the Polo Grounds, NYC

Robert Hofstadterand Rudolf Ludwig Mössbauer win the Nobel Prize in Physics for their pioneering studies of electron scattering in atomic nuclei and discoveries concerning the structure of the nucleon
David Lean's film "Lawrence of Arabia", based on life ofT. E. Lawrenceand starringPeter O'Toole, premieres at Odeon Leicester Square (Academy Awards Best Picture 1963)
6-year old Donny Osmond's singing debut on "TheAndy WilliamsShow"
Karl ZieglerandGiulio Nattareceive the 1963 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for their work on the technology of high polymers
Leonard Bernsteinpremieres his Third Symphony "Kaddish" with the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra, in Tel Aviv, Israel
Dorothy Hodgkinis the first British woman to be awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for work on penicillin and vitamin B12
Nobel Peace Prize presented to Dr.Martin Luther King Jr.in Oslo, Norway
CBS officially renames CBS-TV Studio 50 in New York (built in 1927 as Hammerstein's Theatre), as "TheEd SullivanTheater" in celebration of the 20th anniversary of his program
Guatemalan authorMiguel Ángel Asturiasis awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature in Stockholm
Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine awarded toRagnar Granit,Haldan Keffer HartlineandGeorge Wald"for discoveries about the make-up of the eye
Joe Frazierbeats Oscar Bonavena in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Soviet novelistAleksandr Solzhenitsynchooses not to claim his Nobel Prize in Literature for fear that the USSR would prevent his return afterwards. Accepts in 1974 after he was deported.
Lucasfilm Ltd. is founded as a film and television production company byGeorge Lucasin San Francisco, California
West German ChancellorWilly Brandtreceives the Nobel Peace Prize
Bruno Sammartinobeats Stan Stasiak, to become WWWF champ for 2nd time
For the first time since 1885, tennis has 2 top-ranked male players - AmericansStan SmithandJimmy Connors
Nobel Peace Prize awarded to IrishmanSeán MacBridefor his human rights work and Japanese Prime MinisterEisaku Satōfor signing Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty
Andrei Sakharov's wifeYelena Bonneraccepts the Soviet dissident's Nobel Peace Prize in Oslo - Sakharov not allowed to leave the country
Terry Funk beatsJack Briscoin Miami Beach, Florida to become NWA wrestling champ
Samuel C. C. Tingis the first person to deliver a Nobel Prize lecture in Mandarin, during the ceremony to award him and Burton Richter the Nobel Prize for Physics for discovering the J/ψ particle
"Superman: The Movie", directed by Richard Donner and starringChristopher Reeve,Marlon Brando,Gene Hackman, and Margot Kidder premieres at the Uptown Theater in Washington, D.C.
Menachem BeginandAnwar Sadataccept the Nobel Peace Prize in Oslo for negotiating peace between Egypt and Israel
Heavyweight Michael Doakes KOsMike Weaverin 1:03 in Las Vegas
Danuta Walesa, wife ofLech Wałęsa, accepts his Nobel Peace Prize
South African ArchbishopDesmond Tutuis presented with his Nobel Peace Prize
"Out of Africa", based on the book by Isak Dinesen, directed bySydney Pollackand starringMeryl StreepandRobert Redfordpremieres in Los Angeles (Best Picture 1986)
Holocaust survivorElie Wieselaccepts 1986 Nobel Peace Prize for being a leading spokesman on the Holocaust
Ivan Lendlrepeats previous year's result with a 6–4, 6–4, 6–4 win overBoris Beckerto claim back-to-back season-ending ATP Masters Grand Prix tennis titles at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine presented toGertrude B. Elion,George H. Hitchingsand James W. Black "for their discoveries of important principles for drug treatment"
ArchitectI. M. Peireceives $5 million for design of Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Museum to be built in Cleveland, Ohio
Martin Lutherpublicly burns Pope Leo X's papal bull 'Exsurge Domine' which demanded that Luther recant his writings
France begins use of Gregorian calendar
Treaty of Paris which ended the Spanish–American War is signed by US President McKinley; US acquires Philippines, Puerto Rico and Guam
Edward VIII signs Instrument of Abdication, giving up the British throne to marry American divorceeWallis Simpson
